creator: gerald@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->closed

Thanks for proposing this patch.  I had considered such a change, but 
am not planning to make this for lang/gcc42. 

You mentiong "bring[ing] gcc42 from ports closer to one from the base 
system" as a goal, alas this needs to work differently:  local changes 
need to be submitted upstream and then we'll automatically inherit them 
for future imports into the base system as well as the lang/gcc ports. 

The discrepancy we are seeing with GCC 4.2 is due to a local change 
that has not been submitted upstream (like others, I'm afraid), only 
for me to independently come up with this patch -- and submit it 
upstream.  This is why GCC 4.3 and later upstream versions have this 
change, as does lang/gcc43. 

Multiple requests to FreeBSD developers for help with GCC, both by 
upgrading the system compiler as well as addressing issues upstream 
have resulted in zero help.  One proposal was to leave the base system 
with an older version and use ports versions for the ports collection. 
For lack of anyone helping with anything else, this seems like the most 
likely course of action right now, and keeping the lang/gcc ports as 
vanilla as possible is fully in line with that. 

Whoever wants some additional fixes or functionality is welcome to use 
lang/gcc43, the current default for USE_FORTRAN=yes.
